mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of cases, or t-stat for return difference is < 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and housing demand channels, boosting aggregate economic activity and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days following macroeconomic shocks.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but forward returns are negative in >50% of recovery-phase observations, or predictive power vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 0.3% monthly change threshold
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold but SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of cases, or signal loses predictive accuracy in low-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicates robust job creation without labor market slack, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth combined with flat UNRATE fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or returns are negative in >55% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and supply chain activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below prior month value for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Manufacturing contraction triggers but SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of instances, or the signal fails to predict downturns in subsequent quarters.
